4188.A Driving score rates your driving based on the 6 criteria below. The better your driving, the higher your score.

4566.Accelerate gradually to keep control of your car and avoid wasting fuel or the battery. Sudden acceleration can lead to loss of traction, particularly on wet or slippery roads. Accelerate smoothly and try to reach your desired speed gradually. Doing so helps maintain vehicle balance and stability and reduces wear on the engine and gearbox. Sudden accelerations can surprise other drivers, who may not notice you.
3493.Acceleration: you’re not a Hamilton or a Schumacher.

"4578.Practise smooth braking by applying steady, gentle pressure on the brake pedal rather than slamming on it. Anticipate stops by looking ahead and slowing down as soon as possible. This reduces the risk of skidding or losing control, especially in wet or icy conditions. Keep a safe following distance (at least 3 seconds behind the vehicle in front) to give yourself enough time to brake safely if the car ahead suddenly stops. But if you must stop in an emergency, apply full brake pressure. Don't worry – you won't break the pedal."

4587.To stay alert, plan for regular breaks every 2 hours or every 160 kilometres. Fatigue can severely impact reaction times and decision-making. During breaks, take the opportunity to stretch your legs and do light exercises to improve circulation and reduce stiffness. Stay hydrated by drinking water and consider a light snack to maintain energy levels. Use breaks to rest your eyes from the strain of constant driving by looking at distant objects. Doing so helps prevent fatigue and maintains your focus. Avoid driving for more than 8-10 hours in a single day. Taking frequent breaks helps maintain focus and reduce the risk of accidents caused by drowsiness.
3894.Excessive trip length: drivers take a break even at the 24 Hours of Le Mans race.

4574.Regularly check your fuel or battery level, especially before long trips. Get into the habit of refilling your tank or recharging your battery when it falls below 25%. Remember — you never know when you might end up in a traffic jam for a long period. A low fuel or battery level could leave you stranded or unable to run essential systems such as heating, cooling, or charging. Be aware of the fuel or charging stations along your route, so plan stops to ensure you never run out of fuel or battery unexpectedly.
3599.Fuel / Battery: your car won’t get anywhere without fuel.

4586.Be aware that visibility is worse at dawn, dusk and at night due to less light and potential glare from oncoming vehicles. Drive slower and be extra vigilant at these times. Be aware of animals crossing the road, especially in rural or forested areas. Slow down in places with “Animals crossing” signs, and scan road verges for movement. During the rush hour, be extra cautious and patient. Expect sudden stops, lane changes, and unpredictable behaviour from other drivers. Keep a safe distance and avoid driving aggressively. Show tolerance to other drivers, and always leave space for emergency vehicles (ambulances, police cars, and fire engines).
3852.Night driving / Rush hour: many animals are active at night or the rush hour.

4555.Remember that higher speeds need longer stopping distances. Sticking to speed limits is not just about avoiding fines, it’s essential to keep you and others safe. Driving at a reasonable speed allows you more time to react to the unexpected. Remember that in certain situations, such as overtaking slower vehicles, temporarily increasing your speed is acceptable.
3627.Speeding: speed isn’t a problem — but stopping suddenly is.
3407.In the lower left-hand edge below the windscreen

"4581.Your vehicle is registered in a fleet and managed by a fleet operator (e.g. your company or employer). The fleet operator is responsible for processing and transferring vehicle data (e.g. geolocation data and maintenance data). The fleet operator has the authority to restrict or override your selected privacy mode settings in the vehicle. In such cases, the data will be transferred to the fleet operator regardless of the current privacy mode settings.
For more information about the data processing when the vehicle is registered in a fleet, please contact your fleet operator."

"4410.Please note — by submitting feedback, you agree that our customer support team may contact you by email or phone.
This will help us get the most out of your feedback."
3796.We would love to hear your feedback — it could help us improve the app.
3705.Thank you for your feedback. You are helping us improve our app.

"4495.Now, an even better map experience!
> Chargers load much faster
> You can display up to 1,000 pins at a time
> When you zoom out, pins near each other will cluster together
Your car data loads much faster too — with a timestamp showing when it was loaded."

"4525.Here's what is new in this version:
* **Test drive** Schedule your test drive with MyŠkoda in just a few taps.
* **Redesigned maps** Our new-look maps make planning your trip even easier.
* **Expanded Pay to Park** Thanks to increased coverage, you can find more parking spaces and pay for them with MyŠkoda. "

"4571.Here's what is new in this version:
* **Exterior temperature** Helps you decide when to use auxiliary heating (for supported vehicles only).
* **Car states (online, standby, etc.)** Now always available at a glance.
* **Route details redesign** Key information is easier to read thanks to a new, fresher layout.
* **Marketing consent** Don’t miss out! For all the latest news and updates, please confirm your consent and country of residence. "

"4561.Here's what is new in this version:
* **Charging statistics** You can now track your charging session history (Elroq/Enyaq).
* **Trip planner** You can now set Powerpass-compatible charging stations as preferred on your route (Elroq/Enyaq).
* **Refresh** Update data with a new pull-down feature.
* **Driver challenges** Become a greener driver and win digital badges! "

"4562.Here's what is new in this version:
* **Trip planning with AI** Discover and explore new destinations with Škoda Adventurer, powered by AI.
* **Driving score** Get rated on your driving skills and become a safer driver.
* **Contacting us** We want to make things easier for you! Therefore, we’ve updated and simplified our process for sharing feedback and reporting issues with your car. "

"Here's what is new in this version:
* **Feature 1** Detail description.
* **Feature 2** Detail description.
* **Feature 3** Detail description.
* **Feature 4** Detail description. "

4225.Most Škoda vehicles come with a limited warranty. To extend your coverage, check out our [extended warranty package](%s).
4403.Most Škoda vehicles come with a limited warranty. If you want to extend your coverage, you have time until delivery to purchase a [extended factory warranty](%s).

4508.Extend your warranty and avoid unexpected service costs. With the purchase of a new vehicle, simply request the [Škoda Extended Warranty](%s) supplemental service and instead of 2 years, you will be covered for up to 5 years. Depending on your preference, there are multiple options available.

"4550.Once you have activated your car, you can use services and features in this app (such as remotely controlling your vehicle or getting the current vehicle status).
When can I start? The activation option is displayed on the home screen of your MyŠkoda app, which you can complete during the delivery of your vehicle. Find out more about [Škoda Connect](%s)."

"4532.Once you've activated your car, you will be able to use services and features in this app (such as remotely controlling your vehicle or getting the latest vehicle status).
When can I begin? The activation option will appear on your app's homescreen — which you can finish after car collection. Learn more about [Škoda Connect](%s)."

4442.Have you already prepared for [Škoda Connect](%s)? Download the MyŠkoda app now and get ready for your vehicle. You can find out what the Connect services offer you in the [availability list](%s).

4563.Once you activate [Škoda Connect](%s) services, you expand the possibilities of your vehicle. The services are divided into logical thematic packages: Infotainment Online (expansion of infotainment functions), Proactive Service (facilitation of car maintenance), and Remote Access (access to the vehicle remotely). The MyŠkoda application serves as the assistant for the entire Škoda Connect services.

4451.Once the [connectivity](%s) activation is ready, you will see a step-by-step enrolment guideline in the App. To complete activation, simply carry out the final step of the process in your car after pickup.

"4449.Škoda offers various charging options. Whether you're on the road or at home, you'll find the right solution. Feel free to find out about public charging options and the possibility of charging at home."
4370.Škoda offers various charging options. Whether on the road or at home, you can find the right solution. Check out the public or home charging options.

4526.Škoda offers various charging options. Whether on the road or at home, you can find the right solution. Please feel free to check charging cards, the public charging options and the option of charging at home. Also check whether you can make use of subsidies for the installation of a wallbox at your place of residence.

"4610.Are you the direct owner of the vehicle? Then take advantage of the [Powerpass](%s) card, which allows you to charge your vehicle practically anywhere throughout Europe. You can choose from 3 tariffs based on the frequency of public charging, and all information on charging and payments will be available to you in a clear mobile application.
Do you have a company vehicle? The [Chargee](%s) card offers a comprehensive electric mobility solution for fleet customers with electrified vehicles and their own charging solutions, including settlement (home, corporate, and public charging) and other services suitable for the operation of corporate fleets in the Chargee mobile application and web interface for fleet managers.
Charging at home is easy thanks to [eMobility](%s) packages that include various types of support from Škoda Auto. From checking your home circuits (Home Check) to installing home wallboxes - Škoda iV Chargers - to purchasing and installing photovoltaic panels, battery storage, and other components to ensure the highest possible energy self-sufficiency for the client."

"4507.Škoda offers various charging options. Whether on the road or at home, you can find the right solution.
Public charging? [Powerpass](%s) is compatible with most public charging providers.
Home charging? The [Škoda iV Charger](%s) is designed to charge your Škoda at home or work."

"4548.Whether you're looking for subscription-based charging, a home charging box, or the nearest charging station for your Škoda, [Clever is your obvious choice](%s).

4576.The [Škoda Prepaid Service](%s) supplemental service saves you the worries and expenses associated with servicing your vehicle. It covers regular service inspections, oil and filter changes, and depending on the selected version, also the replacement of parts subject to normal wear and tear. You can prepay for the service for 5 years or according to selected mileage, but please note that it can only be purchased for a vehicle before the first service inspection within 2 years of being put into operation (whichever comes first).
4241.We all use our cars in different ways, so we offer different types of [service and repair subscriptions for your Škoda](%s).

4546.[Service & Maintenance Agreement](%s) makes your car a value investment. With it, you ensure the best resale value for your new car. For a monthly fee, you get the right to have your car serviced and repaired at any authorized Škoda Service partner in Finland without any extra charge. A list of all Škoda Service partners can be found [here](%s).
4064.Customise our [Premium Care Services Package](%s) by choosing the timeframe and mileage limit.

4465.Live your Škoda to the fullest without worrying about periodic maintenance. Subscribe to one of our Extended Drive maintenance plans and avoid price fluctuations and extra costs. Learn more about [Extended Drive](%s).

"4484.With the Škoda Plus warranty, which is standard in Austria, you are already well covered for 5 years/100,000 km. If you would like to have a higher coverage of the manufacturer's warranty in terms of mileage or duration, please contact your Škoda dealer."

4522.Škoda cars come with a 2-year warranty together with Škoda genuine spare parts and accessories. High-voltage batteries for fully electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles are granted 8 years or 160 000 km, depending on which limit is reached first. You can find more information on our [warranty webpage](%s).
